methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Velo3D is a public-stage additive manufacturing company in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector that has raised over $300M in total capital. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 peers: 1 public company and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics operating in adjacent robotics and manufacturing verticals.

Traditional peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single category—below the minimum threshold for reliable median-based triangulation. A broader all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given Velo3D's specialized market position in 3D printing infrastructure.

Without sufficient narrow peer alignment to apply quantitative multiples-based methods, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range anchored to comparable company data. Key uncertainties limiting the analysis include the heterogeneity of business models and unit economics across the private comp set, the limited public market reference (one ticker), and timing lag in private valuations. A complete valuation framework would require either (1) deeper financial disclosure from the private peer set, (2) application of discounted cash flow methodology if company projections are available, or (3) forward-looking revenue or EBITDA multiples specific to additive manufacturing segments.
